repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

Neovim had no build support for the `wasm32-emscripten` target. In addition, building for WASM is not
straightforward as it requires Emscripten specific compiler configuration, a proper sysroot and libc
setup so all dependencies find the correct wasm compatible headers instead of native system headers,
and adjustments to dependencies that assume a native environment. Additionally, LuaJIT does not
support the WASM architecture at all, so the build must fall back to standard Lua 5.1.

## Solution

This PR introduces initial build support for the `wasm32-emscripten` target by making emcc the sole
linker and keeping Zig's role limited to compilation only.

### Changes

-  Detect `wasm32-emscripten` target and branch build logic 
- Add emscripten sysroot option and libc path setup so dependencies find the correct wasm compatible system headers instead of native ones
- Default `cross_compiling=true` for wasm since it always cross-compiles from the host
-  LuaJIT does not support the wasm architecture so `lua5.1` is used instead
- Add emscripten include paths to all dependencies so their C sources find emscripten headers instead of native system headers
- **Guard `addInstallArtifact`**: wrap with `if (!is_wasm)` so Zig only compiles source files into `.a` archives for wasm and never runs its own linker, making emcc the one and only link step
- Make emcc depend directly on the compile step instead of the install step so it receives raw unlinked object archives
- Add `-Wl,--no-gc-sections`, `-sLINKABLE=1`, and `-Wl,--export-all` to prevent `wasm-ld` from eliminating unreferenced symbols at link time

### Known Limitations

- `setjmp`/`longjmp` is not yet fully functional due to Zig compiling dependencies outside the emcc pipeline.
- Several OS-level `uv_*` functions depend on OS features that do not exist in a browser environment. Their linker warnings are   suppressed for now via `-sERROR_ON_UNDEFINED_SYMBOLS=0` and minimal stubs are provided that return `-1` or `0` to allow the build to complete and the wasm module to load.

comment_data.body: instead of using the install path directly in a RunStep, it is better to use `emcc.addOutputFileArg("nvim.js")`  and then `b.addInstallPath` on the result path. This ensures the incremental rebuild logic keeps working.
I don't think this is needed, `nvim_exe.root_module` is still `nvim_mod`
I don't think this is needed either. it is fine to use the `zig build nvim_bin` command for now if the runtime is unsupported.
would be more idiomatic with `b.addWriteFiles()` , compare `test_config_step`. then `.add()` gives a LazyPath instead but where `emscripten_libc_path` is used a LazyPath is fine
what is this for? `abi` should already be detected as `.gnu` if the host is glibc-based.

since this is currently the only way to use `is_wasm`, I'd suggest instead `const s = emscripten_sysroot orelse @panic("-Demscripten-sysroot required")` or something like that, also saves a level of indentation.
please run `zig fmt build.zig` , it will fix this and some other formatting issues. could also squash the commits, then ready to merge:)

comment_regular_data.body: how to use this? surely this deserves a note in BUILD.md ?

> how to use this? surely this deserves a note in BUILD.md ?

The current build command is:

```sh
zig build nvim_bin \
  -Dtarget=wasm32-emscripten \
  -Demscripten-sysroot=$EMSDK/upstream/emscripten/cache/sysroot \
  -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall
```
I'm not sure whether it should be documented as a supported target yet, since there are still known limitations around setjmp/longjmp support. But, the build does successfully produce a WASM output, so I think we could add a short section to BUILD.md describing the build process and explicitly mark the target as experimental. Wdyth?

>  I think we could add a short section to BUILD.md describing the build process and explicitly mark the target as experimental.

yes 👍 


also in favor of including the artifact in our [nightly assets](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/releases/tag/nightly)

> also in favor of including the artifact in our [nightly assets](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/releases/tag/nightly)

I'm currently working on handling the remaining limitations. Once things are stable and behaving correctly, it would be great to consider including the WASM build in the nightly artifacts.
